title: Account Intelligence Brief
description: Unified output schema for the fine-tuned account intelligence model. One model, one schema, six surfaces (meeting_prep, qbr, handoff, renewal_alert, onboarding, escalation). The model emits only the sections relevant to the requested surface; the rest are declared via `empty_sections` so absence is explicit. This is the training contract — every example in the dataset conforms to this shape.

_meta.description: Optional example-level annotation — NOT model output. The training pipeline strips this before scoring; it lives alongside the brief to capture construction notes, synthetic vs real provenance, redaction status, etc. Shape is intentionally free-form: this is annotation, not contract.

account.description: Account identity. Stable across surfaces — same fields whether the brief is a QBR or an escalation.

name.description: Canonical account name as it appears in Salesforce.

type.description: SF Account.Type. Free-text on the SF side — common observed values: 'End User' (most customer rows), 'Customer', 'Prospect', 'Partner', 'Other'. Null is legitimate when SF hasn't categorized the row. Drives downstream surface behavior — e.g. Prospect tilts toward research vs internal data.

cid.description: Zenlayer Customer ID. NULL for non-customer rows.

sf_account_id.description: Salesforce Account.Id (18-char). Anchor for source attribution.

surface.description: Which use case this brief is for. The model is conditioned on this — same input data, different surface = different sections emphasized.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the brief was synthesized.

executive_summary.description: 1–3 sentences capturing the most important state of this account for this surface. The single 'if you read nothing else' line. Always present; never empty.

confidence.description: Model's own assessment of how grounded the brief is in the source data. Lets consumers route low-confidence briefs to human review.

rationale.description: 1 sentence explaining the overall score. Low typically means missing key sources (no SF data, stale activity).

per_section.description: Section-key → high|medium|low. Optional — only include when section-level confidence varies meaningfully.

empty_sections.description: Section keys the model deliberately checked and found no signal for. Declaring absence is the anti-hallucination guard — if a section isn't here AND isn't populated, the model failed to consider it.

sources_used.description: Distinct upstream source types that contributed to this brief. Lets consumers show 'sourced from SF + Teams + Knowledge' chips. Source types not present here MUST NOT appear in any inline citation.

must_address.description: The 'what to drive in this surface' layer. Each entry is one topic, mentioned once. 3–5 max; rank by urgency descending. Empty = nothing material, which is honest. Used by: meeting_prep, escalation, renewal_alert.

topic.description: Short label, ideally citing a real artifact (deal name, case #, contact).

what_happened.description: 1–2 sentences of factual context — dates, amounts, status. No narrative.

open_in_meeting.description: The specific question/move for THIS surface. Concrete and verbatim-ready.

watch_for.description: The landmine — what NOT to do or what signal to read. Null/empty if none.

pipeline.description: Active deal state. Used by: meeting_prep, qbr, handoff, renewal_alert.

total_active_mrr.description: Formatted dollar amount of open opps. Null when none.

next_step.description: SF Opportunity.NextStep field. Often null. When present, the rep's explicit note on the next move for this deal.

support_health.description: Open support / escalation state. Used by: escalation, qbr, handoff, renewal_alert.

priority.description: SF Case.Priority. Null is legitimate — bot-filed cases (RBL notices, abuse-bot tickets) frequently come back with no priority set. The model should pass null through rather than guessing a tier.

trend.description: Whether ticket volume / severity is trending well over the look-back window.

relationship_map.description: Who matters and how. champions/blockers/neutral describe DISPOSITION toward us; decision_makers describes AUTHORITY (often orthogonal — a decision-maker can be a champion, a blocker, or neutral). new_contacts surfaces first-seen-in-window faces. Used by: handoff, onboarding, qbr, renewal_alert.

decision_makers.description: Contacts with explicit decision authority — sign-off on contracts, capacity commitments, architecture choices. Orthogonal to champions/blockers (a decision-maker can be either or neither in disposition). Use sparingly: only when authority is established in the source data, not inferred from title alone.

new_contacts.description: Contacts surfaced for the first time in this brief's look-back window.

recent_activity.description: Factual record of recent touchpoints. Used by: meeting_prep, qbr, handoff, onboarding, escalation.

type.description: Activity category. opportunity_event covers SF opp lifecycle (created, stage-change, closed); case_event covers SF case lifecycle (created, escalated, closed). internal_note is a rep-authored observation outside SF — typically from a handoff dossier or QBR-prep memo.

external_signal.description: Signal from outside Zenlayer's own systems. Used by: meeting_prep, qbr, renewal_alert.

relevance.description: Why this matters for the brief's surface.

internal_chatter.description: Signal from internal Teams channels + SharePoint that's NOT in SF.

knowledge_context.description: Internal playbooks / case studies relevant to the surface. Used by: meeting_prep, onboarding, qbr.

pricing_history.description: Quote / deal pricing context. Used by: meeting_prep, qbr, renewal_alert. Two arrays here: recent_quotes is QUOTE-LEVEL (pricing_quotes table rows with display_id like SQUSWyyyymmddNNN); period_breakdown is BILLING-LEVEL (pricing_historical rows, per period × product_line × dc_code). Don't conflate them.

summary.description: 1 sentence on the account's quote + billing pattern. Period-over-period trajectory or quote cadence.

recent_quotes.description: Quote-level records from pricing_quotes — one entry per quote the rep authored. NOT per billing period; use period_breakdown for that.

display_id.description: SQUSWyyyymmddNNN-style ID from pricing_quotes.display_id. Quote-level, not line-item.

summary.description: 1-line description of what was quoted — multi-line / multi-metro deals condense to one phrase.

amount.description: Total of the quote (formatted, e.g. '$42,000 MRR'). Null if MRR isn't the right summary.

period_breakdown.description: Period-over-period billing roll-up from pricing_historical. One entry per (period × product_line × dc_code) combination. Used by QBR briefs to show trajectory.

renewal.description: Renewal posture. Required for renewal_alert; useful for qbr.

timeline.description: Chronological key events. Used by: handoff, onboarding — gives a new owner the narrative arc.

event.description: 1 sentence: what happened, why it mattered.

asks_and_next_steps.description: Concrete actions / decisions outstanding. Used by every surface.

owner.description: Who needs to do this — name or role.

landmines.description: Things to AVOID / watch for in this surface. Used by: meeting_prep, handoff, renewal_alert.

landmine.description: What not to do, in one phrase. Field name is 'landmine' (NOT 'description' or 'issue').

severity.description: Optional — how much damage the landmine causes if stepped on. Matches the severity scale on renewal.risk_signals.

talking_points.description: Suggested conversation threads — different from must_address (which is the hard agenda). Used by: meeting_prep, qbr.

carryover.description: Lines from a prior brief on this account that are still live. Used by: meeting_prep (refresh), qbr (period-over-period).

meeting_opener.description: ONE sentence of strategic framing for how to open the conversation. Used by: meeting_prep only.

note.description: One-line context on how they fit.

sources.description: Optional per-contact source attribution. Useful when a contact's role or engagement signal comes from a specific upstream record (e.g. 'first appeared on email thread X' or 'title pulled from LinkedIn profile').

Source attribution for a specific claim. Lets us trace any factual line back to its upstream record. Optional on every section but strongly encouraged — claims without sources are downgraded in the confidence score.

ref.description: Upstream identifier — case number, opp ID, message permalink, doc URL, etc. NULL when the source type doesn't carry stable IDs (e.g. some Teams chatter).

snippet.description: Optional ≤200-char excerpt for ground-truth verification. Not for rendering to the user — for training-data audit and eval.
